Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Look up the current user's identity using contacts_get_current_user_details to confirm Dmitri Volkov (dmitri.volkov@moveops.com).
OE 2: Search for Dmitri's manager using contacts_search_contacts with a query like "Samira" or "engineering manager" (or similar) to find Samira Tariq (samira.tariq@moveops.com).
OE 3: Search for the finance lead using contacts_search_contacts with a query like "finance" or "Head of Finance" (or similar) to find Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com).
OE 4: Search for Elena Rostova's email using contacts_search_contacts with a query like "Elena" (or similar) to find elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 5: Locate the expense tool project in Linear using linear_list_projects, then retrieve all associated tickets using linear_list_issues. Relevant tickets include ENG-211 through ENG-220 and their statuses (in_progress, todo, backlog).
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query like "false approval" or "financial exposure" or "expense audit" (or similar) to find the Mosaic false approval exposure (~$885) and Vectral false rejection details.
OE 7: Search email using search_emails with a query like "stipend audit" or "duplicate receipt" (or similar) to find Dmitri's audit email to Marcus detailing three flagged TechComfort Supply transactions: Kevin Tran (Vectral, $89, paid), Andre Washington (Mosaic, $89, pending), Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, $45, pending), total ~$223.
OE 8: Retrieve Marcus's reply in the same thread (via get_email_by_id or search_emails) confirming Kevin Tran's $89 was disbursed and the other two were placed on administrative hold.
OE 9: Search email using search_emails with a query like "verification request" or "suspicious receipt" (or similar) to find that Mina emailed Rachel Whitfield (Vectral) and Emeka emailed Tamara Byrd (Mosaic) and Tessa Moreno (BrightLoop), with no replies in the system.
OE 10: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query like "vendor billing" or "billing discrepancy" (or similar) to find Chloe Vance's breakdown of the Heartland Movers Q1 bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042): $12,800 billed, $8,000 legitimate, $4,800 overbilling across 3 disputed moves, bill flagged as DISPUTED with payment hold.
OE 11: Optionally verify the Heartland bill status in QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills with a query related to "Heartland" (or similar).
OE 12: Send the technical breakdown to Samira Tariq via send_email (to samira.tariq@moveops.com) or via conversations_add_message (Slack DM to Samira), covering ticket statuses, config fix progress, and audit findings.
OE 13: Send the financial exposure summary to Marcus Thorne via send_email (to marcus.thorne@moveops.com) or via conversations_add_message (Slack DM to Marcus), covering the ~$885 false approvals, ~$223 suspicious receipts, and ~$4,800 Heartland overbilling.
OE 14: Send the executive brief to Elena Rostova via send_email (to elena.rostova@moveops.com) or via conversations_add_message (Slack DM to Elena), with a concise summary of remediation status and overall financial exposure.
OE 15: Check Samira's and Dmitri's calendars for the week of March 11 to 15, 2026 using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to or calendar_search_events.
OE 16: Create a calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) with samira.tariq@moveops.com as attendee, ~60 minutes, within March 11 to 15, 2026.

OE 14: Send the executive brief to Elena Rostova via send_email (to elena.rostova@moveops.com) or via conversations_add_message (Slack DM to Elena), with a concise summary of remediation status and overall financial exposure.

1 The model must use a contacts tool (e.g., contacts_search_contacts, contacts_get_current_user_details, or contacts_get_contacts) to look up contact information.
tool selection
 ✗✓—✓✗✗ 40%
✗Run #1No contacts tool (contacts_search_contacts, contacts_get_current_user_details, contacts_get_contacts) was used. The agent used linear_get_user to look up user emails instead, which is not a contacts tool.
✓Run #2The agent called contacts_search_contacts for both 'Dmitri' and 'Samira', retrieving their contact details including email addresses before sending communications.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The agent made contacts_search_contacts calls for 'Samira' (tool call 26), 'Marcus' (tool call 27), and 'Elena' (tool call 28) to look up contact information before sending emails.
✗Run #5No contacts tool (contacts_search_contacts, contacts_get_current_user_details, etc.) was called in the trajectory. The agent used linear_list_users to obtain email addresses for Marcus Thorne and other staff, which is a workaround but not a contacts tool.
✗Run #6No contacts tools (contacts_search_contacts, contacts_get_current_user_details, etc.) were called at any point in the trajectory. The agent proceeded directly with hardcoded email addresses without looking up contact information.
2 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues, linear_list_projects, or linear_get_issue) to retrieve engineering ticket data.
tool selection
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1linear_list_issues was called (and Bash was used to read the result file), and subsequently linear_list_teams, linear_list_projects, and linear_get_user were also called. Linear data was clearly retrieved and used.
✓Run #2The agent made multiple linear_list_issues calls with queries for 'expense tool', 'audit', 'config', 'stipend', 'duplicate', 'validation', and also queried by team 'team_engineering', plus linear_list_teams and linear_get_user.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The agent made multiple linear_list_issues calls (tool calls 6, 7, 8, 19, 20, 21, 22) and a linear_list_teams call (tool call 16) and linear_list_issues with team filter (tool call 29), retrieving ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-219, ENG-220, and more.
✓Run #5linear_list_issues was called (call #13) and returned data covering all engineering tickets. Additional Bash reads of the large result file parsed out 20 engineering issues including ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-219, ENG-220 with their statuses.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues 7 times with various queries (expense tool, audit, config, duplicate, stipend, pipeline, validation) and also called linear_list_teams and linear_get_user. Linear data was actively retrieved to inform ticket statuses.

11 A message was delivered to Samira Tariq (via send_email to [samira.tariq@moveops.com](mailto:samira.tariq@moveops.com) or via conversations_add_message to her Slack DM) and the tool returned success, with the body referencing the status of at least 3 engineering tickets from the expense tool project by their identifiers (e.g., ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-220, or similar) or by descriptions that unambiguously map to specific tickets.
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com includes a detailed table of engineering tickets with identifiers ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-219 (plus new tickets ENG-397 through ENG-402) with their statuses — well over the 3-ticket minimum.
✓Run #2The email to Samira contained a full Engineering Ticket Status table referencing 11 tickets with their status: ENG-214 (IN PROGRESS), ENG-215 (IN PROGRESS), ENG-216 (IN PROGRESS), ENG-217 (IN PROGRESS), ENG-212 (IN PROGRESS), and more. Well exceeds the 3-ticket minimum.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The email to Samira (tool call 44) references ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-218, and newly filed ENG-397 through ENG-404, well exceeding the 3-ticket threshold with explicit status information.
✓Run #5The email to samira.tariq@moveops.com contained a comprehensive engineering ticket status section covering ENG-214 (in_progress), ENG-215 (in_progress), ENG-217 (in_progress), ENG-219 (todo), ENG-220 (todo), plus the 7 new discrepancy tickets ENG-397 through ENG-403.
✓Run #6The email sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com was described as a full technical breakdown including ticket statuses. The Linear queries returned ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, and ENG-220 with statuses, and the agent's final response explicitly references all these tickets by number in the summary sent to Samira.
12 The final response, or a message sent to any recipient (via send_email or conversations_add_message), reports the Mosaic Robotics false approval financial exposure at approximately $885.
outcome
 ✗✗—✗✓✗ 20%
✗Run #1No mention of $885 Mosaic false approval exposure in the final response or any message sent. The stipend section covers $89 paid (Kevin Tran), $134 on hold, $223 total — but the $885 Mosaic false approval figure is entirely absent.
